headline: Five senses to come back to now
subtext: Grounding for when symptoms pull you into overwhelm.
steps: Name five things you can see, then four you can feel.
Then three you can hear, and two you can smell.
Finish with one thing you can taste, and breathe.
caption:
When symptoms or worry pull you out of the moment, your senses can bring you back.

Try this: name five things you can see, four you can feel, three you can hear, two you can smell, and one you can taste. It gives a racing mind something steady to hold, and signals safety to the body.

In chronic pain, fibromyalgia, IBS, tinnitus and long COVID, overwhelm tends to amplify symptoms. A grounding tool you can use anywhere helps take the edge off.

Keep it in your pocket for hard moments. More support at https://neurowaze.health/start-my-recovery

#ChronicPain #Grounding #NervousSystem #Fibromyalgia #IBS #Tinnitus #LongCovid #MindBody #PainRecovery #Neurowaze
first_comment: Grounding through the five senses is a widely used way to ease moments of overwhelm and bring attention back to the present. Find more activities in the Neurowaze Resources area: https://neurowaze.health
